prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `a096bf4d`.
codex_response:
Implemented the new ARC2 task package for `a096bf4d`. The rule is: treat each 4x4 tile’s 2x2 interior slots independently, and whenever the same non-default color appears twice in the same slot along a tile row or tile column, fill the default-colored tiles between them with that color. The Sonnet hint was discarded because it invents a nonexistent fourth training example and misstates the mechanism as row-template copying; the Opus hint matched the official examples and was used as the starting hypothesis. No new shared primitive was needed.

The task package is in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_a096bf4d/__init__.py#L1), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_a096bf4d/verifier.py#L1), [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_a096bf4d/generator.py#L1), and [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_a096bf4d/notes.md#L1). The generator builds the same zero-separated tile lattice family as the official task, chooses one shared base tile, and then places 2-3 distinct horizontal/vertical propagation segments over distinct interior slots so the outputs stay visually on-family.

Validation passed end to end: `4/4` official examples and `1000/1000` generated examples. Generated artifacts are at [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/a096bf4d/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/a096bf4d/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/a096bf4d/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/a096bf4d/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/a096bf4d/previews/preview_03.png). I also opened a preview sheet and the samples look consistent with the official task family.
